questions.original_question: LTIC向Geely UK Limited转让LTIC股权的转让对价是多少？
questions.answer:
关于LTIC股份从LTIC转让给Geely UK Limited的考虑，根据2024年10月1日发布的信息，LTIL与Geely UK Limited签署了股份出售和转让协议，LTIL同意出售800,000股LTIC股份给Geely UK Limited，交易总金额为10,880,000欧元。交易完成后，Geely UK Limited将持有LTIC 80%的股份。这一交易旨在进一步巩固Geely在路特斯科技中的战略布局，并支持其在全球智能豪华出行领域的发展。

以上信息基于2025年4月30日之前的知识。
rewritten_question: What is the consideration for the transfer of LTIC shares from LTIC to Geely UK Limited?

rag_info.chunk_content: Lotus Technology Innovative Limited (the “LTIL”), a wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Technology Inc., recently entered into a share sale and transfer agreement (the “Share Transfer Agreement”) with Geely UK Limited (“Geely”), pursuant to which LTIL agrees to sell, and Geely agrees to purchase, 800,000 shares in Lotus Tech Innovation Center GmbH (“LTIC”), a company incorporated in Germany and a wholly-owned subsidiary of LTIL, for an aggregate principal amount of EUR 10,880,000 (the “Transaction”). The Transaction closed simultaneously with the signing of the Share Transfer Agreement. Upon completion of the Transaction, Geely beneficially owns 80% of LTIC’s share capital. A copy of the Share Transfer Agreement is included in this current report on Form 6-K as Exhibit 10.1, and the foregoing description of the Share Transfer Agreement is qualified in its entirety by reference thereto. Share Sale and Transfer Agreement, dated September 27, 2024, by and between Lotus Technology Innovative Limited and Geely UK Limited.
rag_info.sub_query: What is the consideration for the transfer of LTIC shares from LTIC to Geely UK Limited?

Geely UK Limited Lotus Technology Innovative Limited SHARE SALE AND TRANSFER AGREEMENT relating to the sale and transfer of shares in Lotus Tech Innovation Center GmbH THIS SHARE SALE AND TRANSFER AGREEMENT (the “Agreement”) is made on September 27, 2024 (the “Signing Date”)
rag_info.sub_query: What is the consideration for the transfer of LTIC shares from LTIC to Geely UK Limited?

rag_info.chunk_content: As consideration for Westwood’s irrevocable commitment to purchase ADSs upon the terms of and subject to satisfaction of the conditions set forth in the ELOC Purchase Agreement, Lotus Technology Corporation issued 700,000 Ordinary Shares to Westwood on September 19, 2024. In September 2024, Lotus Technology Corporation entered into a share sale and transfer agreement with Geely UK Limited, pursuant to which Lotus Technology Corporation sold 800,000 shares in Lotus Tech Innovation Center GmbH, or LTIC, a company incorporated in Germany and a wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Technology Corporation, for an aggregate principal amount of EUR 10,880,000. Immediately upon completion of the transaction, Geely UK Limited beneficially owned 80% of LTIC’s share capital. On November 7, 2024, Lotus Technology Corporation entered into a bond subscription agreement, or the Bond Subscription Agreement, with Kershaw Health Limited, a private limited company incorporated in England and Wales, or the Subscriber, pursuant to which the Subscriber agrees to subscribe for and purchase, and Lotus Technology Corporation agrees to issue and sell to the Subscriber, a senior bond due 2029, or the 2029 Bond, with the principal amount of US$500,000,000, at a subscription price of 100% of the principal amount of the Bond. As of the date of this annual report, the transaction has not been closed yet.
rag_info.sub_query: What is the consideration for the transfer of LTIC shares from LTIC to Geely UK Limited?

rag_info.chunk_content: In connection with the transaction, 50% equity interests of one of Lotus Technology's subsidiaries will be pledged to Geely HK. On November 7, 2024, Lotus Technology entered into the Bond Subscription Agreement with Kershaw Health Limited, the Subscriber, pursuant to which the Subscriber agrees to subscribe for and purchase, and Lotus Technology agrees to issue and sell to the Subscriber, a senior bond due 2029, or the 2029 Bond, with the principal amount of US$500,000,000, at a subscription price of 100% of the principal amount of the Bond. As of the date of this annual report, the transaction has not been closed yet. In September 2024, Lotus Technology Investment Limited (LTIL) entered into a share sale and transfer agreement with Geely UK Limited, pursuant to which LTIL sold 80% equity in Lotus Technology International Company (LTIC) for an aggregate principal amount of EUR 10,880,000. Immediately upon completion of the transaction, Geely UK Limited beneficially owned 80% of LTIC’s share capital. On September 29, 2024, Lotus Technology's subsidiary, Hangzhou Kingway Technology Co., Ltd., or Kingway, entered into an exchangeable note agreement with an investor. Pursuant to the agreement, Kingway is entitled to issue exchangeable notes, or the Kingway Exchangeable Notes, of RMB600,000,000 to obtain financing from the investor. The repayments of the Kingway Exchangeable Notes were guaranteed by the ultimate shareholders of Kingway in mainland China, i.e., Wuhan Lotus Technology Co., Ltd., a wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Technology.
rag_info.sub_query: What is the consideration for the transfer of LTIC shares from LTIC to Geely UK Limited?

rag_info.chunk_content: Concurrently with the execution of the Purchase Agreement, Lotus Technology entered into a Registration Rights Agreement with Westwood pursuant to which Lotus Technology agreed to file and have filed a registration statement to register the resale by Westwood of ADSs that may be issued to Westwood by Lotus Technology under the ELOC Purchase Agreement. Each issuance by Lotus Technology under the ELOC Purchase Agreement is subject to a maximum amount equal to 50% of the average daily trading volume in the ADSs on Nasdaq during the five trading days immediately preceding the day Westwood receives a valid purchase notice and subject to a US$100 million cap. The purchase price of ADSs that Lotus Technology may direct Westwood to purchase will be equal to 97% of the lowest daily volume-weighted average price during the three consecutive trading days beginning on the date that a purchase notice is delivered by Lotus Technology to Westwood. As consideration for Westwood’s irrevocable commitment to purchase ADSs upon the terms of and subject to satisfaction of the conditions set forth in the ELOC Purchase Agreement, Lotus Technology issued 700,000 Ordinary Shares to Westwood on September 19, 2024. In September 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. entered into a share sale and transfer agreement with Geely UK Limited, pursuant to which Lotus Technology Inc. sold 800,000 shares in Lotus Tech Innovation Center GmbH, or LTIC, a company incorporated in Germany and a wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Technology Inc., for an aggregate principal amount of EUR 10,880,000.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology Innovative Limited (the “LTIL”), a wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Technology Inc., recently entered into a share sale and transfer agreement (the “Share Transfer Agreement”) with Geely UK Limited (“Geely”), pursuant to which LTIL agrees to sell, and Geely agrees to purchase, 800,000 shares in Lotus Tech Innovation Center GmbH (“LTIC”), a company incorporated in Germany and a wholly-owned subsidiary of LTIL, for an aggregate principal amount of EUR 10,880,000 (the “Transaction”). The Transaction closed simultaneously with the signing of the Share Transfer Agreement. Upon completion of the Transaction, Geely beneficially owns 80% of LTIC’s share capital. A copy of the Share Transfer Agreement is included in this current report on Form 6-K as Exhibit 10.1, and the foregoing description of the Share Transfer Agreement is qualified in its entirety by reference thereto. Share Sale and Transfer Agreement, dated September 27, 2024, by and between Lotus Technology Innovative Limited and Geely UK Limited.

metadata.title_summary:
title: LTIC Share Transfer Agreement 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of the LTIC Share Transfer Agreement**

Lotus Technology Innovative Limited (LTIL), a wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Technology Inc., has executed a Share Transfer Agreement with Geely UK Limited (Geely) for the sale of 800,000 shares in Lotus Tech Innovation Center GmbH (LTIC), a German company that is also a wholly-owned subsidiary of LTIL. The total transaction amount for this share transfer is EUR 10,880,000. 

The transaction was finalized on September 27, 2024, coinciding with the signing of the Share Transfer Agreement. As a result of this transaction, Geely now possesses 80% of LTIC's share capital. The details of the Share Transfer Agreement are documented in this report on Form 6-K as Exhibit 10.1, and the description provided here is subject to the complete terms outlined in that exhibit.

This agreement marks a significant strategic move for both LTIL and Geely, reflecting Geely's increased stake in LTIC and reinforcing its position in the market.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Concurrently with the execution of the Original Merger Agreement, on January 31, 2023, Lotus Technology Corporation (LTC) entered into a put option agreement with each of Geely and Etika, pursuant to which each of Geely and Etika is granted the right to require LTC to purchase all of the equity interests held by each of Geely and Etika in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, the parent company of Lotus UK, at a pre-agreed price which will be calculated based on the total revenue of Lotus Global Investment Limited (LGIL) for the year of 2024 adjusted to exclude net debt, at a future date during the period from April 1, 2025 to June 30, 2025 and contingent upon satisfaction of certain pre-agreed conditions.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Put Option Agreements 
summary: On January 31, 2023, LTC finalized a put option agreement with Geely and Etika, complementing the Original Merger Agreement. This arrangement allows Geely and Etika to compel LTC to buy their equity stakes in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, the parent company of Lotus UK. The purchase price will be based on LGIL's total revenue for 2024, adjusted for net debt, and the transaction can take place between April 1, 2025, and June 30, 2025, provided specific pre-agreed conditions are met.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Concurrent with the execution of the Original Merger Agreement, on January 31, 2023, Lotus Technology Corporation (LTC) entered into a put option agreement with each of Geely and Etika, pursuant to which each of Geely and Etika is granted the right to require LTC to purchase all of the equity interests held by each of Geely and Etika in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, the parent company of Lotus UK, at a pre-agreed price which will be calculated based on the total revenue of Lotus Global Investment Limited (LGIL) for the year of 2024 adjusted to exclude net debt, at a future date during the period from April 1, 2025, to June 30, 2025, and contingent upon satisfaction of certain pre-agreed conditions.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Put Option Agreements 
summary: On January 31, 2023, LTC entered into put option agreements with Geely and Etika as part of the Original Merger Agreement. These agreements provide Geely and Etika with the right to compel LTC to purchase their equity interests in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, which is the parent company of Lotus UK. The purchase price for these equity interests will be determined based on the total revenue of LGIL for the fiscal year 2024, with adjustments made to exclude net debt. The options can be exercised within a specified timeframe, from April 1, 2025, to June 30, 2025, and their execution is contingent upon the fulfillment of certain pre-agreed conditions. This arrangement is designed to ensure that both Geely and Etika have a clear exit strategy while aligning their interests with LTC's performance in the upcoming fiscal year.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Under the terms of the agreement, Lotus Technology received a non-exclusive, perpetual, irrevocable, and non-sublicensable license for the electric automotive chassis and autonomous driving technology platform, referred to as the “Geely License,” with a consideration of US$288.9 million. The Geely License is contractually restricted to be used in certain of Lotus Technology's R&D projects. Lotus Technology concludes that the Geely License has no alternative future use; therefore, the cost of the license has been expensed as research and development expenses in the consolidated and combined statement of comprehensive loss for the year ended December 31, 2021. In November 2021, Lotus Technology obtained the trademark licenses from Group Lotus Limited, a related party controlled by Geely Holding, by issuing 47,995,443 ordinary shares to LGIL, a related party controlled by Geely Holding. On December 2, 2021, Lotus Technology Innovative Limited, a subsidiary of Lotus Technology, entered into an equity transfer agreement, pursuant to which Lotus Technology Innovative Limited agreed to acquire 100% equity interest in Lotus Tech Innovation Centre GmbH from a related party, Geely UK Limited, at a consideration of US$15.5 million, which was settled in June 2022.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Related Party Transactions 
summary: ### Comprehensive Summary of Related Party Transactions

The "Related Party Transactions" chapter outlines the company's extensive financial interactions with related parties over the years, particularly focusing on the sale of sports cars, BEV lifestyle models, auto parts, and consulting services. 

#### Revenue from Related Party Transactions
- **Service Revenue**: The company generated revenue from services provided to related parties amounting to:
  - **2023**: US$12.6 million
  - **2022**: US$8.3 million
  - **2021**: US$3.3 million
- **Product Sales**: Sales of products to related parties were:
  - **2023**: US$3.4 million
  - **2022**: US$23 thousand
  - **2021**: None

#### Accounts Receivable and Payments
- As of December 31, 2023, accounts receivable from related parties totaled **US$22.4 million**, up from **US$8.5 million** in 2022.
- The company made payments on behalf of related parties totaling:
  - **2023**: US$4.0 million
  - **2022**: US$244 thousand
  - **2021**: None
- Related parties collected **US$3.7 million** in 2023.

#### Loans and Prepayments
- Unsecured short-term loans to related parties were:
  - **2023**: US$1.2 million
  - **2022**: US$2.3 million
- Interest income from these loans was recognized at:
  - **2023**: US$122 thousand
  - **2022**: US$24 thousand
- Prepayments for vehicle purchases to related parties amounted to:
  - **2023**: US$21.4 million
  - **2022**: US$6.1 million
- An expected credit loss provision for these prepayments increased from **US$12 thousand** on January 1, 2023, to **US$23 thousand** by December 31, 2023.

#### Purchases from Related Parties
- Total purchases from related parties were significant, amounting to:
  - **2023**: US$852.6 million (with **US$549.9 million** recognized as cost of goods sold)
  - **2022**: US$44.3 million
  - **2021**: US$14.3 million
- Research and development expenses related to these purchases were:
  - **2023**: US$134.7 million
  - **2022**: US$167.0 million
  - **2021**: US$47.4 million
- Equipment and software purchases from related parties totaled **US$11.2 million** in 2023 and **US$42.8 million** in 2022.
- The company incurred short-term lease costs of **US$765 thousand** in 2023.

#### Agreements and Investments
- In 2023, the company established a distribution agreement with Lotus Cars Limited, recording a payable of **US$23.0 million** for distribution rights.
- The company had previously entered into a license agreement for the Geely License, expensing **US$288.9 million** as R&D costs in 2021.
- Additionally, equity interests were acquired in related entities, including a **US$15.5 million** transaction with Geely UK Limited completed in June 2022.
- The fair value of a convertible note purchased from ECARX Holding Inc. was **US$3.3 million** as of December 31, 2023.

Overall, the transactions with related parties reflect significant financial interactions that have notably impacted both revenues and expenses across multiple years, underscoring the importance of these relationships in the company's financial landscape.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Concurrent with the execution of the Original Merger Agreement, on January 31, 2023, Lotus Technology Corporation (LTC) entered into a put option agreement with each of Geely HK and Etika, pursuant to which each of Geely HK and Etika is granted the right to require LTC to purchase all of the equity interests held by each of Geely HK and Etika in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, the parent company of Lotus UK, at a pre-agreed price which will be calculated based on the total revenue of Lotus Global Investment Limited (LGIL) for the year of 2024 adjusted to exclude net debt, at a future date during the period from April 1, 2025 to June 30, 2025 and contingent upon satisfaction of the LAT Put Option Exercise Condition. As of December 31, 2024, the Put Option Exercise Condition had been satisfied. On April 14, 2025, Geely HK exercised its put option, requiring LTC to purchase 51% of the equity interests in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd. Closing of the Geely HK put option could be subject to approvals of governmental authorities in various jurisdictions and is currently expected to take place around the end of 2025.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Put Option Agreements 
summary: On January 31, 2023, LTC entered into put option agreements with Geely HK and Etika, enabling them to require LTC to purchase their equity interests in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, the parent company of Lotus UK. The purchase price will be determined based on LGIL's total revenue for 2024, adjusted for net debt, and the options can be exercised between April 1 and June 30, 2025, provided certain conditions are met. By December 31, 2024, these conditions were fulfilled. Subsequently, on April 14, 2025, Geely HK exercised its option to sell 51% of its equity interests in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd. The completion of this transaction is expected to require governmental approvals and is projected to occur by the end of 2025.

The Geely License is contractually restricted to be used in certain R&D projects of Lotus Group. Lotus Group concludes that the Geely License has no alternative future use; therefore, the cost of the license has been expensed as research and development expenses in the consolidated and combined statement of comprehensive loss for the year ended December 31, 2021. (vii) In November 2021, Lotus Group obtained the trademark licenses (Note 7) from Group Lotus Limited, a related party controlled by Geely Holding, by issuing Lotus Group’s 47,995,443 ordinary shares to LGIL, a related party controlled by Geely Holding. (viii) On December 2, 2021, Lotus Group, through its subsidiary, Lotus Technology Innovative Limited, entered into an equity transfer agreement, pursuant to which Lotus Technology Innovative Limited agreed to acquire 100% equity interest in Lotus Tech Innovation Centre GmbH from a related party, Geely UK Limited, at the consideration of US\$15,512, which was settled in June 2022.

metadata.title_summary:
title: NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED AND COMBINED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS (All amounts in thousands, except for share and per share data) 
summary: ### Comprehensive Summary of Related Party Transactions and Financial Obligations

As of December 31, 2023, the Group has engaged in various related party transactions and financial obligations that significantly impact its consolidated and combined financial statements. Below is a detailed summary of these transactions, categorized by type and including relevant financial figures.

#### 1. Accounts Payable and Current Liabilities

**Purchases from Related Parties:**
- The Group's purchases from related parties, which include sports cars, BEV lifestyle models, auto parts, and consulting services, have increased substantially:
  - **2023:** US$852,623
  - **2022:** US$44,347
  - **2021:** US$14,259
- These purchases were recognized as cost of goods sold:
  - **2023:** US$549,893
  - **2022:** US$839
  - **2021:** US$331
- As of December 31, inventories recorded were:
  - **2023:** US$248,331
  - **2022:** US$21,880
- Accrued expenses included amounts due for office materials and services:
  - **2023:** US$27,660
  - **2022:** US$8,684
- Accounts payable for these purchases stood at:
  - **2023:** US$340,419
  - **2022:** US$5,770

**Research and Development (R&D) Expenses:**
- R&D expenses from related parties were as follows:
  - **2023:** US$134,721
  - **2022:** US$167,012
  - **2021:** US$47,442
- Amounts due for R&D included in accrued expenses:
  - **2023:** US$217,256
  - **2022:** US$162,942

**Equipment and Software Purchases:**
- Purchases from related parties for equipment and software were recorded as:
  - **2023:** US$11,223
  - **2022:** US$42,798
  - **2021:** US$6,255
- Accrued expenses included amounts due:
  - **2023:** US$19,786
  - **2022:** US$8,366

**Miscellaneous Expenses:**
- Related parties incurred various expenses totaling US$14,514 and US$2,952, with amounts due included in accrued expenses:
  - **2023:** US$1,605
  - **2022:** US$2,952

**Short-term Leases:**
- Short-term lease costs incurred were:
  - **2023:** US$765
  - **2022:** US$347
  - **2021:** US$243
- Payables for short-term leases included in accrued expenses:
  - **2023:** US$323
  - **2022:** US$272

**Deposits from Related Parties:**
- Deposits received from related parties were:
  - **2023:** US$251
  - **2022:** US$1,584
- These amounts are included in accrued expenses and other non-current liabilities.

#### 2. Significant Agreements and Transactions

**Distribution Agreement:**
- On January 31, 2023, the Group entered into a distribution agreement with LCL for the exclusive global distribution of certain vehicle models, with a payable of US$23,047 included in accrued expenses.

**Loan and Interest:**
- A one-year unsecured loan of US$10,211 was borrowed from a related party in 2019 and repaid in August 2022, with interest expenses recorded for 2022 and 2021.

**Lease Agreements:**
- Right-of-use assets recognized from lease agreements were:
  - **2023:** US$12,166
  - **2022:** US$214
  - **2021:** US$1,333
- Lease liabilities paid were:
  - **2023:** US$269
  - **2022:** US$98
  - **2021:** US$545

**License Agreement:**
- A license agreement with Zhejiang Liankong was established on March 12, 2021, for electric automotive technology at a cost of US$288,948, expensed as R&D.

**Trademark Licenses:**
- Trademark licenses were obtained from Group Lotus Limited in November 2021 through share issuance.

**Equity Transfer Agreement:**
- The Group acquired a 100% equity interest in Lotus Tech Innovation Centre GmbH from Geely UK Limited for US$15,512, settled in June 2022.

**Convertible Note Purchase:**
- A convertible note of US$10,000 was purchased from Ecarx, which was converted to shares upon listing. Fair values recorded were US$3,326 as of December 31, 2023, and US$8,411 as of December 31, 2022.

**Manufacturing Agreement:**
- A manufacturing agreement with Geely Auto for electric vehicle production commenced on June 21, 2022, outlining responsibilities for design, production, and quality control.

This summary provides a comprehensive overview of the Group's related party transactions and financial obligations, highlighting significant figures and agreements that shape its financial landscape as of December 31, 2023.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Pursuant to the terms of the agreements, if the consummation of the Business Combination occurs prior to the maturity date, all of the then outstanding principal amount and any accrued but unpaid interest (as applicable) of the notes will be automatically converted into LTC Ordinary Shares upon the consummation of the Business Combination. In January 2023, Lotus Technology issued put options to each of Geely International (Hong Kong) Limited (“Geely HK”) and Etika at nil consideration. Pursuant to the put option agreements, each of Geely HK and Etika will have an option to require Lotus Technology to purchase the equity interests held by Geely HK and Etika in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, the immediate parent of LGIL, between April 1, 2025 and June 30, 2025, at a pre-agreed price. The exercise price will be settled with LTC Ordinary Shares. In November 2022, the former VIE’s subsidiary, Hangzhou Lightning Speed Technology Co., Ltd. (“Lightning Speed”), entered into an exchangeable note agreement with an investor. Pursuant to the agreement, Lightning Speed is entitled to issue exchangeable notes to obtain financing from the investor. Each tranche of the exchangeable notes is scheduled to mature on the five-year anniversary date of issuance. With the consent of the investor, each tranche of the exchangeable notes is eligible for two extensions, and each extension shall not exceed one year.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Liquidity and Capital Resources 
summary: The summary of cash flows and working capital provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance across various periods, emphasizing cash inflows and outflows, liquidity trends, and operational efficiency. The data covers two six-month periods ending June 30 for 2023 and 2022, as well as full years ending December 31 for 2022 and 2021, detailing key metrics in operating, investing, and financing activities, along with the effects of exchange rate changes.

Key findings indicate a notable increase in net cash used in operating activities, with losses escalating significantly in both the six-month and full-year comparisons. In contrast, cash outflows from investing activities showed a decrease in the latest six-month period, while the previous full year experienced a shift from positive inflows to negative outflows. Financing activities, however, provided substantial cash inflows, reflecting a strong reliance on financing to support operations.

The impact of exchange rate fluctuations was detrimental, contributing to losses in cash balances. The company's cash position revealed a decline in net cash and restricted cash in 2023 compared to the previous year, despite an increase in opening cash balances. The company has historically funded its operations through various financing means, including convertible notes and capital contributions, but now faces challenges in securing additional liquidity for ongoing operations.

To address these challenges, the company is exploring multiple strategies for securing funding, including potential external financing linked to a Business Combination and issuing new convertible notes. However, there is uncertainty regarding the success of these strategies and the company's ability to continue operations amid ongoing profitability issues and negative cash flows.

Additionally, the company has entered into various agreements related to convertible notes and exchangeable notes, which may impact future financing needs and operational strategies. The company generates revenue in RMB and anticipates ongoing challenges related to foreign currency conversion and regulatory approvals, which could further complicate its financial stability.

In summary, while the company has a strong cash position relative to previous years, it faces significant cash flow challenges, increased operating losses, and reliance on financing, necessitating strategic improvements and liquidity management to ensure future operational viability.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Geely Holding, a global mobility technology group with a proven track record in seeding BEV brands, has successfully incubated and revitalized a series of world-renowned brands with attractive financial profiles, including Volvo, Polestar, LYNK&CO., and Zeekr. Positioned as the only Geely Holding-affiliated brand with sports car DNA, Lotus Technology has received comprehensive support from Geely Holding in manufacturing, supply chain, research and development, logistics infrastructure, and human capital, and is in the process of transforming from a British sports car company to a global pioneer of high-performance electric vehicles to bridge the gap between traditional sports cars and a new generation of electric vehicles. Lotus Technology expects the business combination with LCAA, a SPAC affiliated with L Catterton, which has a strategic relationship with LVMH, to provide significant support in consumer insights and brand collaboration that will enable the company to effectively raise brand awareness globally. L Catterton has an exclusive relationship for consumer-focused private equity with LVMH and Financière Agache, the main investment company of Bernard Arnault, LVMH’s chairman and chief executive officer, and his family.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Overview 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of the Overview Chapter**

We are a pioneering luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer operating under the iconic British brand "Lotus," which boasts over 70 years of racing heritage. Since our acquisition by Geely Holding in 2017, we have been transitioning from a traditional sports car company to a luxury lifestyle BEV maker, leveraging Geely's extensive support in manufacturing, supply chain, and research and development (R&D).

The global luxury BEV market, defined as vehicles with a Manufacturer's Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) exceeding $80,000, is projected to experience significant growth, with a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 35% from 2021 to 2031, ultimately reaching nearly 1.9 million units by 2031. Currently, this market is underserved, featuring only about 10 luxury BEV models compared to over 100 internal combustion engine (ICE) luxury models.

Our first fully electric model, the Eletre, launched in 2022, is a luxury E-segment SUV powered by our proprietary 800-volt Electric Performance Architecture (EPA). The Eletre is available in three versions, with the Eletre R delivering an impressive 905 horsepower and achieving 0 to 100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds. We aim to achieve a 100% BEV product portfolio by 2027, having launched our second lifestyle vehicle, the Emeya, in September 2023, with deliveries scheduled to commence in March 2024. Future product launches include a D-segment SUV in 2025 and a BEV lifestyle sports car in 2026.

Our R&D capabilities serve as a competitive strength, supported by five facilities located in the U.K., Germany, and China. These facilities focus on developing advanced technologies, including a leading Advanced Driver Assistance System (ADAS). We manufacture our BEV models in partnership with Geely, utilizing their state-of-the-art facilities in Wuhan, China, with a planned annual production capacity of 150,000 units.

We employ a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, operating 215 stores globally as of December 31, 2023, with plans to expand to over 300 by 2025. In 2023, we successfully delivered 6,970 vehicles, encompassing both lifestyle and sports car models. Our collaboration with L Catterton, which has connections to LVMH, is anticipated to enhance our brand awareness and consumer insights, although no formal agreements with LVMH have been finalized as of this prospectus.

This strategic positioning within the luxury BEV market, combined with our innovative product offerings and robust R&D capabilities, positions us well for future growth and success in the evolving automotive landscape.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Concurrently with the execution of the Original Merger Agreement, on January 31, 2023, Lotus Technology Corporation (LTC) entered into a Put Option Agreement with each of Geely and Etika, pursuant to which each of Geely and Etika is granted the right to require LTC to purchase all of the equity interests held by each of Geely and Etika in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, the parent company of Lotus UK, at a pre-agreed price which will be calculated based on the total revenue of Lotus Group International Limited for the year of 2024 adjusted to exclude net debt, at a future date during the period from April 1, 2025 to June 30, 2025 and contingent upon satisfaction of certain pre-agreed conditions. See “Agreements Entered Into in Connection with the Business Combination    —     Put Option Agreements.” Concurrently with the execution of the Original Merger Agreement, on January 31, 2023, Lotus Technology Corporation (LTC) entered into the Put Option Agreement with each of Geely and Etika, pursuant to which each of Geely and Etika is granted the right, but not the obligation, to require LTC to purchase all of the equity interests held by each of Geely and Etika in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, the parent company of Lotus UK, at a pre-agreed price which will be calculated based on Lotus Group International Limited’s total revenue for the year of 2024 adjusted to exclude net debt.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Put Option Agreements 
summary: On January 31, 2023, LTC entered into a Put Option Agreement with Geely and Etika in conjunction with the Original Merger Agreement. This agreement allows Geely and Etika to require LTC to repurchase their equity interests in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, the parent company of Lotus UK. The repurchase price will be determined based on the total revenue of Lotus Group International Limited for the year 2024, adjusted for net debt. The put options can be exercised between April 1 and June 30, 2025, contingent upon Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd and its subsidiaries selling over 5,000 vehicles in 2024.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Each of the put options granted to Geely and Etika may only be exercised during the period from April 1 to June 30, 2025, and is contingent upon satisfaction of the condition that the total number of vehicles sold by Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd and its subsidiaries exceeds 5,000 in 2024. The put options granted to Geely and Etika can be exercised independently from each other, and the exercise of each put option is not conditioned upon the exercise of the other.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Put Option Agreements

This agreement allows Geely and Etika to require LTC to repurchase their equity interests in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, the parent company of Lotus UK. The repurchase price will be determined based on the total revenue of Lotus Group International Limited for the year 2024, adjusted for net debt. The put options can be exercised between April 1 and June 30, 2025, contingent upon Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd and its subsidiaries selling over 5,000 vehicles in 2024.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Inc. 
summary: On September 27, 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. executed a Share Sale and Transfer Agreement concerning the sale and transfer of shares in Lotus Tech Innovation Center GmbH. This agreement involves Geely UK Limited and Lotus Technology Innovative Limited. The document was signed by Alexious Kuen Long Lee, who serves as the Director and Chief Financial Officer of Lotus Technology Inc. Certain details within the agreement have been redacted, as they are considered non-material and treated as private or confidential by the company, indicated by the symbol “[REDACTED].” This transaction reflects Lotus Technology Inc.'s strategic initiatives and partnerships aimed at enhancing its operational capabilities and market presence.

dated as of June 24, 2024 between  
Lotus Technology Inc. and Geely International (Hong Kong) Limited This CONVERTIBLE NOTE PURCHASE AGREEMENT (this “Agreement”), dated as of June 24, 2024, is entered into by and between (i) Lotus Technology Inc., an exempted company with limited liability organized and existing under the laws of the Cayman Islands (the “Company”), and (ii) Geely International (Hong Kong) Limited, a company limited by shares incorporated under the laws of Hong Kong (the “Purchaser”).

metadata.title_summary:
title: CONVERTIBLE NOTE PURCHASE AGREEMENT 
summary: Geely International (Hong Kong) Limited, a subsidiary of Geely Automobile Holdings, is dedicated to expanding the company's global footprint in the automotive sector. It manages international investments, partnerships, and promotes Geely's vehicles and technologies worldwide, emphasizing innovation, sustainability, and customer experience. Additionally, a Convertible Note Purchase Agreement dated June 24, 2024, has been established between Lotus Technology Inc. and Geely International, detailing the investment terms for convertible notes issued by Lotus.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: As consideration for Westwood’s irrevocable commitment to purchase ADSs upon the terms of and subject to satisfaction of the conditions set forth in the ELOC Purchase Agreement, Lotus Technology Corporation issued 700,000 Ordinary Shares to Westwood on September 19, 2024. In September 2024, Lotus Technology Corporation entered into a share sale and transfer agreement with Geely UK Limited, pursuant to which Lotus Technology Corporation sold 800,000 shares in Lotus Tech Innovation Center GmbH, or LTIC, a company incorporated in Germany and a wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Technology Corporation, for an aggregate principal amount of EUR 10,880,000. Immediately upon completion of the transaction, Geely UK Limited beneficially owned 80% of LTIC’s share capital. On November 7, 2024, Lotus Technology Corporation entered into a bond subscription agreement, or the Bond Subscription Agreement, with Kershaw Health Limited, a private limited company incorporated in England and Wales, or the Subscriber, pursuant to which the Subscriber agrees to subscribe for and purchase, and Lotus Technology Corporation agrees to issue and sell to the Subscriber, a senior bond due 2029, or the 2029 Bond, with the principal amount of US$500,000,000, at a subscription price of 100% of the principal amount of the Bond. As of the date of this annual report, the transaction has not been closed yet.

metadata.title_summary:
title: A. History and Development of the Company 
summary: The Lotus BEV business, founded in 2018, operates through various entities in China, the UK, and Germany, including Wuhan Lotus Cars and Lotus Tech UK. A significant restructuring in 2023 eliminated the previous Variable Interest Entity (VIE) structure, streamlining operations under subsidiaries in mainland China. Key developments include the establishment of LTC in the Cayman Islands in 2021, the acquisition of equity interests in Lotus Tech UK and Lotus GmbH, and trademark licensing agreements with Group Lotus Limited. 

In March 2022, LTC executed a 10-for-1 stock split, increasing its ordinary shares significantly. The company continued its restructuring into 2023, moving away from the VIE model. In February 2024, LTC completed a business combination with LCAA, leading to the trading of its American Depositary Shares (ADSs) on Nasdaq. Subsequent financial activities included a private placement of convertible bonds with Geely HK and agreements for share purchases and bond subscriptions, indicating ongoing capital raising efforts. 

By September 2024, LTC had entered into agreements for significant share sales and bond subscriptions, including a $500 million bond agreement with Kershaw Health Limited. Future plans involve the acquisition of a controlling interest in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, which is crucial for manufacturing and consultancy services related to Lotus' sports cars and hypercars. The company’s principal executive office is located in Shanghai, China, with additional information available through its corporate website and the SEC's site.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: In connection with the transaction, 50% equity interests of one of Lotus Technology's subsidiaries will be pledged to Geely HK. On November 7, 2024, Lotus Technology entered into the Bond Subscription Agreement with Kershaw Health Limited, the Subscriber, pursuant to which the Subscriber agrees to subscribe for and purchase, and Lotus Technology agrees to issue and sell to the Subscriber, a senior bond due 2029, or the 2029 Bond, with the principal amount of US$500,000,000, at a subscription price of 100% of the principal amount of the Bond. As of the date of this annual report, the transaction has not been closed yet. In September 2024, Lotus Technology Investment Limited (LTIL) entered into a share sale and transfer agreement with Geely UK Limited, pursuant to which LTIL sold 80% equity in Lotus Technology International Company (LTIC) for an aggregate principal amount of EUR 10,880,000. Immediately upon completion of the transaction, Geely UK Limited beneficially owned 80% of LTIC’s share capital. On September 29, 2024, Lotus Technology's subsidiary, Hangzhou Kingway Technology Co., Ltd., or Kingway, entered into an exchangeable note agreement with an investor. Pursuant to the agreement, Kingway is entitled to issue exchangeable notes, or the Kingway Exchangeable Notes, of RMB600,000,000 to obtain financing from the investor. The repayments of the Kingway Exchangeable Notes were guaranteed by the ultimate shareholders of Kingway in mainland China, i.e., Wuhan Lotus Technology Co., Ltd., a wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Technology.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Cash flows and working capital 
summary: The cash flow summary for the years 2022 to 2024 provides a detailed analysis of the organization's financial movements, emphasizing operating, investing, and financing activities. In 2024, the organization reported a net cash outflow of $848,520 from operating activities, an increase from previous years, while investing activities also saw significant outflows totaling $579,440. However, financing activities generated a substantial net inflow of $1,459,527, reflecting the organization's reliance on various financing mechanisms, including bank loans and shareholder contributions.

The cash positions at the beginning of 2024 were $427,135, a decrease from prior years, but by the end of 2024, cash and restricted cash rose to $484,937. Exchange rate fluctuations positively impacted cash balances in 2024, contrasting with negative effects in the previous two years. Overall, the net cash increase for 2024 was $57,802, a turnaround from the decrease experienced in 2023.

Several significant financing agreements were established in 2024, including a $70 million convertible bond sale to Geely HK, a $500 million senior bond subscription with Kershaw Health Limited, and an exchangeable note agreement totaling RMB 600 million by Hangzhou Kingway Technology. The organization also engaged in a share sale agreement with Geely UK Limited and secured short-term loans backed by intellectual property.

Despite these financing efforts, the organization faces challenges related to profitability and cash flow management, raising concerns about its ability to sustain operations without effective strategies for securing additional funding. The company is exploring various options, including cutting expenses and pursuing new loans or equity financing, amidst uncertainties in global financial markets.

Additionally, the organization’s revenue is primarily denominated in RMB, which may complicate capital expenses and foreign currency conversions due to regulatory requirements in mainland China. The summary underscores the importance of effective cash management and strategic planning to navigate financial challenges and ensure continued operational viability.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Immediately upon completion of the transaction, Geely UK Limited beneficially owned 80% of LTIC’s share capital. On November 7, 2024, Lotus Technology entered into a bond subscription agreement, or the Bond Subscription Agreement, with Kershaw Health Limited, a private limited company incorporated in England and Wales, or the Subscriber, pursuant to which the Subscriber agrees to subscribe for and purchase, and Lotus Technology agrees to issue and sell to the Subscriber, a senior bond due 2029, or the 2029 Bond, with the principal amount of US$500,000,000, at a subscription price of 100% of the principal amount of the Bond. As of the date of this prospectus, the transaction has not been closed yet. On November 29, 2024, Lotus Technology entered into a share buyback agreement, or the Share Buyback Agreement, with Meritz, pursuant to which Meritz agrees to sell and surrender, and Lotus Technology agrees to repurchase, 17,500,000 ADSs, or the Repurchased ADSs. Ordinary Shares underlying the Repurchased ADSs constitute a part of the 50,000,000 Ordinary Shares, or the Meritz Shares, issued by Lotus Technology to Meritz concurrently with the closing of the Business Combination pursuant to the Meritz Subscription Agreement. The total consideration payable by Lotus Technology for the buyback equals the amount that provides Meritz with a 12.5% internal rate of return, calculated for the period between the Closing Date and the date of the closing of the buyback (referred to as the “Buyback Closing” and the date of the Buyback Closing, the “Buyback Closing Date”) on US$175 million.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Business Updates 
summary: In 2024, the company reported impressive preliminary results, delivering 12,065 vehicles, which represents a year-on-year growth of over 70%, with China contributing about 25% of global deliveries. A significant development occurred on June 24, 2024, when a convertible note purchase agreement was signed with Geely HK, allowing Geely to acquire convertible notes totaling a specified amount in U.S. dollars. These notes, which amount to RMB800 million, will mature on June 22, 2025, and carry an interest rate of SORF plus 3.35% per annum, with the option for Geely to convert them into Ordinary Shares or American Depositary Shares (ADSs) after 30 trading days.

On September 16, 2024, the company entered into an ELOC Purchase Agreement with Westwood Capital Group LLC, permitting Westwood to purchase up to $350 million in ADSs over a three-year period, subject to specific conditions. This agreement included a Registration Rights Agreement to facilitate the resale of the ADSs. Additionally, the company issued 700,000 Ordinary Shares to Westwood as part of the arrangement.

In September 2024, a share sale agreement was made with Geely UK Limited, resulting in the sale of 800,000 shares of the wholly-owned subsidiary, Lotus Tech Innovation Center GmbH, for EUR 10,880,000, granting Geely UK an 80% stake in LTIC. A bond subscription agreement with Kershaw Health Limited was also established on November 7, 2024, for a senior bond of $500 million due in 2029, although this transaction is still pending.

On November 29, 2024, a share buyback agreement with Meritz was finalized, involving the purchase of 17,500,000 ADSs, which will allow Meritz to achieve a 12.5% internal rate of return on its $175 million investment. This buyback will also release several obligations under the original subscription agreement with Meritz. 

In terms of leadership changes, Mr. Teik Huat Ooi resigned as a director on December 6, 2024, and Mr. Abdul Farid Bin Alias was appointed to the position effective January 22, 2025. Looking ahead, on February 28, 2025, another convertible note purchase agreement with Geely HK is anticipated, involving $70 million in convertible notes maturing in 2027, with similar terms regarding conversion and interest.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Immediately upon completion of the transaction, Geely UK Limited beneficially owned 80% of LTIC’s share capital. On November 7, 2024, Lotus Technology entered into a bond subscription agreement, or the Bond Subscription Agreement, with Kershaw Health Limited, a private limited company incorporated in England and Wales, or the Subscriber, pursuant to which the Subscriber agrees to subscribe for and purchase, and Lotus Technology agrees to issue and sell to the Subscriber, a senior bond due 2029, or the 2029 Bond, with the principal amount of US$500,000,000, at a subscription price of 100% of the principal amount of the Bond. As of the date of this prospectus, the transaction has not been closed yet. On November 29, 2024, Lotus Technology entered into a share buyback agreement, or the Share Buyback Agreement, with Meritz, pursuant to which Meritz agrees to sell and surrender, and Lotus Technology agrees to repurchase, 17,500,000 ADSs, or the Repurchased ADSs. Ordinary Shares underlying the Repurchased ADSs constitute a part of the 50,000,000 Ordinary Shares, or the Meritz Shares, issued by Lotus Technology to Meritz concurrently with the closing of the Business Combination pursuant to the Meritz Subscription Agreement. The total consideration payable by Lotus Technology for the buyback equals the amount that provides Meritz with a 12.5% internal rate of return, calculated for the period between the Closing Date and the date of the closing of the buyback (Lotus Technology refers to such closing as the “Buyback Closing” and the date of the Buyback Closing, the “Buyback Closing Date”) on US$175 million.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Business Updates 
summary: In 2024, our company achieved significant growth, delivering 12,065 vehicles, a remarkable increase of over 70% compared to the previous year, with China contributing around 25% of global deliveries. A convertible note purchase agreement was established with Geely HK on June 24, 2024, involving the acquisition of convertible notes totaling a specified amount in U.S. dollars, which Geely HK is raising through a private placement of RMB800 million. These notes, maturing on June 22, 2025, carry an interest rate of SORF plus 3.35% per annum, with conversion options into Ordinary Shares or American Depositary Shares (ADSs) starting 30 trading days post-issuance.

On September 16, 2024, we entered into an ELOC Purchase Agreement with Westwood Capital Group LLC, allowing for the purchase of up to $350 million in ADSs over 36 months, with specific purchase limitations and pricing conditions. Concurrently, we issued 700,000 Ordinary Shares to Westwood as part of this agreement. In September, we also sold 800,000 shares of our German subsidiary, Lotus Tech Innovation Center GmbH, to Geely UK Limited for EUR 10,880,000, resulting in Geely UK acquiring an 80% stake in LTIC.

On November 7, 2024, we established a bond subscription agreement with Kershaw Health Limited for a senior bond of $500 million, which is still pending finalization. Later that month, a share buyback agreement with Meritz was initiated, involving the repurchase of 17,500,000 ADSs linked to a previous business combination, structured to ensure a 12.5% internal rate of return for Meritz. This buyback is still under discussion.

In terms of leadership changes, Mr. Teik Huat Ooi resigned as director on December 6, 2024, and Mr. Abdul Farid Bin Alias was appointed effective January 22, 2025. Finally, on February 28, 2025, we entered into another convertible note purchase agreement with Geely HK for $70 million, with similar terms to previous agreements, including a maturity date of February 28, 2027, and a pledge of 50% equity interests in one of our subsidiaries.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Concurrently with the execution of the Purchase Agreement, Lotus Technology entered into a Registration Rights Agreement with Westwood pursuant to which Lotus Technology agreed to file and have filed a registration statement to register the resale by Westwood of ADSs that may be issued to Westwood by Lotus Technology under the ELOC Purchase Agreement. Each issuance by Lotus Technology under the ELOC Purchase Agreement is subject to a maximum amount equal to 50% of the average daily trading volume in the ADSs on Nasdaq during the five trading days immediately preceding the day Westwood receives a valid purchase notice and subject to a US$100 million cap. The purchase price of ADSs that Lotus Technology may direct Westwood to purchase will be equal to 97% of the lowest daily volume-weighted average price during the three consecutive trading days beginning on the date that a purchase notice is delivered by Lotus Technology to Westwood. As consideration for Westwood’s irrevocable commitment to purchase ADSs upon the terms of and subject to satisfaction of the conditions set forth in the ELOC Purchase Agreement, Lotus Technology issued 700,000 Ordinary Shares to Westwood on September 19, 2024. In September 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. entered into a share sale and transfer agreement with Geely UK Limited, pursuant to which Lotus Technology Inc. sold 800,000 shares in Lotus Tech Innovation Center GmbH, or LTIC, a company incorporated in Germany and a wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Technology Inc., for an aggregate principal amount of EUR 10,880,000.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: LTIC is a world-class engineering facility in electric mobility. As a part of Lotus Technology's international research and development network, LTIC develops new products and solutions for a new era of premium performance driving and participates in the development of Lotus Technology's high-end technology, such as regional development for advanced driver-assistance systems (ADAS), electric powertrain architecture, digital vehicle dynamics, vehicle hardware, and intelligent cabin systems. LTIC adopts an agile organizational structure where its members can work in different teams based on the focuses of various phases of the project. LTIC operates in three main areas: ● The global development team oversees global products, including, among others, digital chassis and digital vehicle dynamics. This team has assisted with component integration, durability testing, certification and homologation, as well as electric vehicle management systems. ● The regional development and delivery team collaborates with the U.K. and China teams and is responsible for global research and development platform deployment and specification development, catering to regional customer needs. This team is also responsible for ensuring compliance of the company's products with legal and regulatory requirements, including, among others, analyzing local legislation, managing data centers, and addressing cybersecurity matters.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Germany 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Germany Chapter: Lotus Technology Innovation Center (LTIC)**

The Lotus Technology Innovation Center (LTIC) stands as a leading engineering facility dedicated to electric mobility, integrated within an expansive international research and development (R&D) network. The center is instrumental in advancing high-end technologies that enhance premium performance driving, focusing on key areas such as Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS), Electric Power Architecture (EPA), digital vehicle dynamics, and intelligent cabin systems. LTIC's agile organizational structure fosters collaboration among team members across various project phases, promoting innovation and efficiency.

The operations at LTIC are organized into three primary areas:

1. **Global Development**: This team is responsible for the development of global products, particularly in digital chassis and vehicle dynamics. Their activities encompass component integration, durability testing, certification, and management of electric vehicle systems, ensuring that products meet high-performance standards.

2. **Regional Development and Delivery**: Collaborating closely with teams in the U.K. and China, this group focuses on deploying global R&D platforms while tailoring specifications to meet regional customer needs. They play a crucial role in ensuring compliance with legal and regulatory requirements, which includes conducting local legislation analyses, managing data centers, and addressing cybersecurity concerns.

3. **Innovation and New Platform Development**: This team is tasked with creating innovative concepts and architectures for future products. They explore emerging technologies and lead advancements in digital vehicle dynamics, positioning LTIC at the forefront of automotive innovation.

In addition to its operations in Germany, LTIC's R&D efforts extend to China, with significant activities concentrated in Wuhan, Shanghai, and Ningbo. These efforts focus on core technology innovations related to electric architecture, charging systems, cloud computing, data processing, system integration, network security, batteries, energy management, electric motors, and intelligent driving technologies. A notable initiative, Lotus Robotics, was established in China to enhance the company's intelligent driving capabilities. This initiative encompasses vehicle product development, software development, sensor technology, cloud and data management, testing, validation, and project management.

Overall, LTIC's commitment to electric mobility and advanced technology development positions it as a key player in the automotive industry, addressing both global and regional market demands while ensuring compliance with regulatory standards.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology Innovation Center (LTIC) is a world-class engineering facility in electric mobility. As a part of Lotus Technology's international research and development network, LTIC develops new products and solutions for a new era of premium performance driving and participates in the development of Lotus Technology's high-end technology, such as regional development for Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS), Electric Powertrain Architecture (EPA), new architecture, digital vehicle dynamics, vehicle hardware, and intelligent cabin systems. As of June 30, 2023, the Lotus Technology Innovation Center (LTIC) had approximately 180 full-time employees. The Lotus Technology Innovation Center adopts an agile organizational structure where its members can work in different working teams based on the focuses of various phases of the project. The Lotus Technology Innovation Center works in three main areas: • The global development team oversees global products, including, among others, digital chassis and digital vehicle dynamics. This team has assisted with component integration, durability testing, certification and homologation, as well as electric vehicle management systems. • The regional development and delivery team collaborates with the UK and China teams and is responsible for global research and development platform deployment and specification development, catering to regional customer needs. This team is also responsible for ensuring compliance of Lotus Technology's products with legal and regulatory requirements, including, among others, analyzing local legislation, managing data centers, and addressing cybersecurity matters. • The innovation and new platform development team is responsible for creating new concepts and new architectures for Lotus Technology's future product pipeline.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Germany 
summary: LTIC is a prominent engineering facility dedicated to electric mobility and is part of a global research and development network. It specializes in creating innovative solutions for high-performance driving, with a strong emphasis on advanced technologies such as Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS), electric powertrains, and intelligent cabin systems. As of mid-2023, LTIC employs approximately 180 staff members and operates with an agile organizational structure that fosters collaboration across project phases. The facility's operations are divided into three main areas: a global development team focused on product integration and testing, a regional development team that tailors R&D efforts to meet local needs and regulatory standards, and an innovation team that explores new technologies for future vehicle concepts. This structure enhances flexibility and adaptability, allowing LTIC to effectively advance vehicle dynamics and digital architecture in the electric mobility sector.

LTIC works in three main areas: • The global development team oversees global products, including, among others, digital chassis and digital vehicle dynamics. This team has assisted with component integration, durability testing, certification and homologation, as well as electric vehicle management systems. • The regional development and delivery team collaborates with the U.K. and China teams and is responsible for global research and development platform deployment and specification development, catering to regional customer needs. This team is also responsible for ensuring compliance of Lotus Technology's products with legal and regulatory requirements, including, among others, analyzing local legislation, managing data centers, and addressing cybersecurity matters. • The innovation and new platform development team is responsible for creating new concepts and new architectures for Lotus Technology's future product pipeline. This team studies new technologies and pioneers advancements in digital vehicle dynamics.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Germany 
summary: LTIC is a prominent engineering facility dedicated to electric mobility and is part of a global research and development network. It focuses on innovative solutions for high-performance driving, with key developments in advanced driver-assistance systems (ADAS), electric powertrain architecture, digital vehicle dynamics, vehicle hardware, and intelligent cabin technologies. The organization employs an agile structure that promotes collaboration across various teams tailored to the specific needs of projects. 

LTIC's global development team is responsible for the creation and integration of products like digital chassis and vehicle dynamics, emphasizing component integration, durability testing, certification, and EV management systems. In parallel, the regional development and delivery team works with counterparts in the U.K. and China to adapt R&D platforms and specifications to meet local customer demands and regulatory requirements, including cybersecurity. Additionally, the innovation and new platform development team focuses on conceptualizing future product architectures and exploring emerging technologies, ensuring flexibility and efficiency in their collaborative efforts throughout different project phases.
